title: Inspection Plan
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of an Inspection Plan.
Learn the components of an Inspection Plan.
Understand inspection characteristics and sampling procedures.
Learn standard SAP Inspection Plan transaction codes.
Understand how Inspection Plans are used during quality inspections.

introduction.title: What is an Inspection Plan?
introduction.content: An Inspection Plan is a Quality Management master data object that defines how quality inspections should be performed. It specifies the inspection operations, characteristics to be checked, sampling procedures, inspection methods and acceptance criteria used during quality inspections.

business_purpose.title: Why is an Inspection Plan Important?
business_purpose.content: Inspection Plans standardize quality inspection procedures across the organization. They ensure every inspector follows the same inspection process, reducing variation and improving product quality.

inspection_plan_components.description: Contains general information about the Inspection Plan.
Defines inspection steps to be performed.
Characteristics that need to be inspected.
Determines how many samples are inspected.
Defines how inspection should be carried out.

integration.purpose: Inspection Plan is assigned to the material.
Characteristics are reused within Inspection Plans.
Inspection results are entered against the defined characteristics.
Inspection Plan provides the basis for quality acceptance.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: For a stainless steel coil, the Inspection Plan defines that inspectors must check thickness, width, surface finish and hardness. The plan specifies the sampling procedure, inspection sequence and acceptance limits to ensure every batch is inspected consistently.

best_practices: Reuse Master Inspection Characteristics whenever possible.
Review Inspection Plans after engineering changes.
important_notes: Inspection Plans are reusable for multiple inspection lots.
Every inspection lot uses the Inspection Plan maintained for the material.
Inspection characteristics should reflect current product specifications.
jsl_notes: Inspection Plans should follow approved JSL quality standards.
Inspectors must ensure the latest Inspection Plan version is used before recording results.
Engineering specification changes should be reflected in the Inspection Plan immediately.
